name: Vulture Peak
description: Vulture Peak, also known as Gijjhakuta in Sanskrit and Pali, is a significant mountain located near Rajgir in the present-day Indian state of Bihar. It holds immense importance in Buddhist tradition as one of the Buddha's favorite places to reside and teach. Many important sutras were delivered on Vulture Peak, including the Lotus Sutra and the Heart Sutra, making it a central location for Mahayana Buddhism. The mountain's name derives from its resemblance to a vulture, or perhaps from the presence of vultures in the area. It was a place of solitude and contemplation, attracting not only the Buddha and his disciples but also other ascetics and spiritual seekers. The serene environment and natural beauty provided an ideal setting for meditation and the dissemination of the Dharma. Vulture Peak is considered one of the eight great holy places of Buddhism and continues to be a site of pilgrimage and reverence for Buddhists worldwide. The mountain represents the Buddha's wisdom and compassion, and its enduring legacy serves as a reminder of the teachings delivered there centuries ago. The peak offered a panoramic view, aiding in both mental clarity and spiritual insight, fostering an environment conducive to profound enlightenment.
subtitle: Sacred Mountain of Buddhist Teachings
significance: One of Buddhism's eight great holy places

keyMyths.title: The Lotus Sutra Discourse
keyMyths.description: The Buddha delivered the Lotus Sutra on Vulture Peak, a pivotal event in Mahayana Buddhism. This sutra emphasizes the potential for enlightenment for all beings.

keyMyths.title: The Heart Sutra Revelation
keyMyths.description: The Heart Sutra, a concise expression of emptiness, was also taught at Vulture Peak. It encapsulates the core principles of wisdom and understanding in Buddhist philosophy.

sources.author: Nakamura, Hajime
